extra.reasoning:
The property requires that flipping back_pain from false to true (all else fixed) never lowers acuity. back_pain appears in exactly one rule, r13 (priority 100), which fires only when 12 inputs (pallor, palpitations, syncope, focal_weakness, stiff_neck, dyspnea, fever, severe_headache, abdominal_pain, confusion, dizziness, back_pain) are all true, and it sets acuity to self_care — the lowest acuity. Consider the input where those 11 non-back_pain conjuncts are all true and leg_swelling is false (leg_swelling is not part of r13's conjunction).

With back_pain = FALSE: r13 does not fire. Several priority-3 rules fire (r1 palpitations, r2 confusion, r3 dyspnea, r4 focal_weakness, r10 severe_headache), all setting acuity := emergent. Highest priority among matching rules is 3, so acuity = emergent.

With back_pain = TRUE (the witness shown): all 12 conjuncts of r13 are now true, so r13 fires at priority 100, which dominates every priority-3 rule, setting acuity := self_care.

Thus setting back_pain true drops acuity from emergent to self_care, lowering it — a direct violation of monotonicity. The witness_assignment gives the back_pain=true state; its back_pain=false counterpart (identical except back_pain=false) yields emergent, demonstrating the violation.
